criteria.evidence: Story 1 has S/T/A/R/L with three distinct actions: 'I designed a 4-arm test... I pre-defined D7 retention as the success metric and instrumented it. Then I made the call to ship...'
criteria.reason: Every one of the 5 stories has all five STAR+L fields, each Action decomposed into three concrete decisions.

criteria.evidence: 'I designed a 4-arm test', 'I made the call to ship', 'I built an LTV model', 'I rebuilt the referral readout'
criteria.reason: Consistent individual ownership in first person; the lone 'We killed it' (Story 2) is preceded by clearly owned 'I ran a holdout'.

criteria.evidence: Real: '22% to 28%', 'dropped activation 6%', '~$1.5M'. Flagged: 'Activated-invitees lift needs a number', Story 4 'no number — this is a self-awareness story'.
criteria.reason: Every number traces to the persona (22-28, 6%, 15%, 1.5M, 4-arm, 6yrs verified). Unquantified results are explicitly flagged, never invented. Story 3 even avoids leading with the 15% vanity metric it argues against.

criteria.evidence: 'Pre-commit to the metric before you see the results, then have the spine to ship the winner over the favorite.'
criteria.reason: Every story ends with a generalizable one-sentence lesson.

criteria.evidence: 'JD -> story map' table mapping 8 question types to primary/backup stories; 'Strengthen before the room: tighten to 90s'.
criteria.reason: 5 stories with deliberate reuse mapped across the role's archetypes; constraint surfaced and coached. Minor density but self-flagged.

criteria.evidence: Story 1 (onboarding 4-arm/ambiguous bet), Story 2 (mandatory-tutorial holdout/judgment), Story 3 (invites->activated-invitees/disagreeing-up); coverage check surfaces missing peer-conflict as an open ask.
criteria.reason: All three expected archetypes covered plus honest-gap and impact; missing people-mgmt/peer-conflict honestly flagged rather than fabricated.

highest_leverage_fix: None required — passing. Optional polish: build-stories could trim the densest stories (1, 3) to verifiably hit the 90s bar rather than only flagging it.
